MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-21-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Defendant Music City Hotel Lps Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEVEL 5 DRYWALL, INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Music City Hotel Lps Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Moving party Level 5 Drywall, Inc., the plaintiff in this case, seeks an order compelling defendant Music City Hotel LLP to respond to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Request for Admissions, and Request for Production of Documents, Set One. The motion is unopposed. The moving papers establish that Plaintiff served this discovery on Music City Hotel and Music City Hotel hasnot responded. Under these circumstances, Music City Hotel has waived its objections to the requests and interrogatories. (CCP 2030.290(a); 2031.300(a).)

The court orders Music City Hotel to provide code-compliant responses to the form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and request for production of documents within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. No objections may be asserted; they have been waived. The court does not order further responses to the RFAs. By companion order, the court has ordered that the matters asserted in the RFAs are deemed admitted. This is a full and complete response to the RFAs by operation of law. No additional remedy is prescribed by CCP 2033.280.

The court shall impose monetary sanctions on a party who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel responses to discovery unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of sanctions unjust. (CCP 2030.290(d), 2031.300c.) The court does not find that Music City Hotel acted with substantial justification. Music City Hotel must pay $1066.75 to Level 5 Drywall Inc. in sanctions within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Motion For Order Establishing Admissions; Request For Sanctions As To Defendant Music City Hotel Lp
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEVEL 5 DRYWALL, INC.'s Motion For Order Establishing Admissions; Request For Sanctions As To Defendant Music City Hotel LP.

Plaintiff seeks an order deeming admitted the truth of matters specified in the requests for admission, set one, that Plaintiff served on defendant Music City Hotel, LLP. Plaintiff's moving papers, which are unopposed, establish that Plaitniff served RFAs on Music City Hotel and that Music City Hotel did not respond. Accordingly, Plaintiff is entitled to the order it seeks. The truth of matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Music City Hotel. (See CCP 2033.280.)

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2023.280(c), provides that it is mandatory for the court to impose sanctions in this instance. The court finds Plaintiff's sanctions request reasonable and awards $716.75 in sanctions, payable by Music City Hotel to Plaintiff. The sanctions shall be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
